lesson.menu_title: On a Call -- Telephone & Video Conferences
lesson.menu_desc: Handle phone calls and lead video conferences with international contacts -- answering, taking messages and fixing tech issues -- with reported speech (He said he would call back; She told me the meeting was moved; He asked if I was available) -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 20 -- On a Call: Telephone & Video Conferences
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marcos Mansour | Aula 20 -- On a Call: Telephone & Video Conferences

listenings.text: Hi Marcos, this is David. I am sorry I missed your call earlier. I am calling to pass on a message from Rachel. She said she had reviewed your proposal, and she told me she was very happy with it. She also asked if you were free for a video call on Thursday. She said she would send a meeting link by email this afternoon. One more thing: she mentioned that the connection was bad during your last call, so she asked you to use headphones this time. Please call me back when you get this, or just reply to confirm Thursday. Thanks, Marcos. Talk soon.

Good morning, Marcos. Here is your call summary for today. You have two messages from yesterday. First, the supplier called while you were out. He said the new prices were ready, and he asked if you could call him back before noon. Second, Karen from the congress left a voicemail. She told me she had joined your video meeting late because her reception was bad, and she apologized. She asked if you could share the slides again. Finally, a reminder: your team said the conference call would start at three o'clock, not two. Please dial in five minutes early. That is all for now. Have a great day.
